channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Former Employee Alleges Special Privileges for Ghislaine Maxwell in Prison
item.description: A former employee at the Federal Prison Camp in Bryan, Texas, where Ghislaine Maxwell is serving her sentence, spoke out about what she claims are exceptional privileges given to Maxwell. Noella Turna...

item.title: A Mother's Sacrifice in the Lviv Ghetto During World War II
item.description: In the harsh winter of 1943, inside the Lviv Ghetto under Nazi occupation, a young Jewish mother faced an unimaginable choice. With starvation, disease, and deportations to death camps threatening her...

item.title: How Shared Backgrounds Influence Immigrant Patients’ Trust in Healthcare
item.description: Many immigrant patients in the U.S. actively seek healthcare providers who share their cultural or linguistic background. This preference is not solely about language; it reflects a desire for deeper ...

item.title: The Complex Geopolitical Dynamics Between Trump and Xi Jinping
item.description: The relationship between former US President Donald Trump and Chinese President Xi Jinping is a delicate mix of formal friendship and deep-seated rivalry, reflecting one of the most consequential geop...

item.title: Echo Network Africa Foundation Plans Expansion to Boost Women’s Empowerment Across Africa
item.description: Echo Network Africa Foundation (ENAF), previously known as Kenya Women Trust Fund (KWFT), is planning to extend its women empowerment initiatives beyond Kenya to the wider East African region and acro...

item.title: The Hanau Shooting: How Racism and Institutional Failures Contributed to Tragedy
item.description: On February 19, 2020, in Hanau, Germany, Vili-Viorel Pǎun attempted to intervene during a mass shooting targeting ethnic minorities, only to be killed after emergency services failed to respond. The a...

item.title: Widespread Atmospheric Silicone Pollution Linked to Vehicle Emissions
item.description: Researchers from Utrecht University and the University of Groningen have identified unexpectedly high levels of methylsiloxanes, a type of silicone-based pollutant, in the atmosphere across urban, rur...

item.title: CBS Anchor Redirected to Taiwan After Visa Issues Prevent Reporting from China
item.description: CBS News faced an unexpected setback when anchor Tony Dokoupil was unable to report from Beijing during President Donald Trump's summit with Chinese President Xi Jinping due to a missing Chinese visa....

item.title: Tanzania Reports Strong Growth in Exports, Manufacturing, and Tourism as Inflation Rises
item.description: During March 2026, Tanzania recorded notable economic growth across multiple sectors. Export activity increased by 12.8%, largely driven by a 38.5% surge in gold exports. The manufacturing sector also...

item.title: Memphis Grizzlies Forward Brandon Clarke Dies at 29
item.description: Brandon Clarke, a seven-year NBA veteran and key player for the Memphis Grizzlies, has tragically passed away at the age of 29. Drafted as the 21st overall pick in 2019, Clarke spent his entire profes...

item.title: Senate Directs Treasury to Resume Meru County Funding Despite French Investor Debt Dispute
item.description: The Kenyan Senate has passed a resolution directing the National Treasury to lift the freeze on funding to Meru County, which was initially imposed due to a dispute involving a foreign debt. The freez...

item.title: Gunfire and Lockdown at the Philippine Senate Amid Arrest Attempt
item.description: On the evening of May 13, 2026, tensions escalated at the Philippine Senate when at least seven gunshots were fired during a lockdown initiated to facilitate an arrest. Acting Senate Sergeant-at-Arms ...

item.title: Gauteng Municipalities Intensify Removal of Illegal Structures to Improve Safety and Order
item.description: Municipalities across Gauteng have stepped up efforts to demolish illegal informal structures and unregulated settlements, citing the need to improve safety, reduce overcrowding, and enhance municipal...

item.title: Prof Dr Sarwar Khawaja honoured with Sitara-e-Imtiaz for contributions to education and entrepreneurship
item.description: LONDON/ISLAMABAD: Prof Dr Sarwar Muhammad Khawaja, a British-Pakistani educationist and entrepreneur, has been awarded the Sitara-e-Imtiaz, one of Pakistan’s highest civilian awards, by President Asif...

item.title: Trump visits China amid global tensions and domestic policy updates
item.description: On May 14, 2026, President Donald Trump arrived in Beijing for the first state visit to China by a US leader in nine years. The summit comes as the world's two largest economies aim to stabilise their...

item.title: Severe storm kills 33 and damages property across five districts of Uttar Pradesh
item.description: On Wednesday, five districts in Uttar Pradesh experienced a severe storm accompanied by heavy rain, resulting in the deaths of at least 33 people, officials reported to PTI. Bhadohi recorded the highe...

item.title: Missouri Supreme Court ruling could allow Democrats to challenge GOP redistricting map
item.description: Missouri's Supreme Court recently upheld the Republican Party's new congressional map, which heavily favors GOP candidates with a 7-1 seat advantage in the House of Representatives. This map could thr...

item.title: Tanzania Police arrest 39 in prostitution crackdown and confiscate illegal substances
item.description: Tanzania Police recently conducted a major operation targeting illegal prostitution activities across the country, leading to the arrest of 39 individuals. During the crackdown, authorities also seize...

item.title: USDA Secretary Brooke Rollins Faces Lawsuit Over Religious Messages in Workplace
item.description: Brooke Rollins, the U.S. Secretary of Agriculture, is facing a lawsuit filed by the National Federation of Federal Employees in California, alleging that she improperly brought religion into the feder...

item.title: Canadian Grand Prix Adjusts Schedule Amid Potential Canadiens Playoff Overlap
item.description: Organizers of the Canadian Grand Prix in Montreal are preparing for an earlier race date, now set for May 24, shifted from mid-June to streamline North American travel and reduce environmental impact....
